description: Known attack vectors against trust systems and their mitigations. Feeds into Phase 2 check_request_safety tool.

attack_vectors.name: Fake History
attack_vectors.description: Agent claims completed tasks that never happened to inflate trust score

mitigation.details: Require verification from the builder or other agents who assigned the task. No self-reported accomplishments count toward trust score without independent confirmation.

open_questions: Who are the trusted third parties initially? Builders are the obvious first verifiers.
Agents verifying agents creates circular trust until the web-of-trust is large enough.
Need concrete verification flow before Phase 2 implementation.

attack_vectors.name: Sybil Attacks
attack_vectors.description: Create multiple fake agents that all vouch for each other to manufacture trust

mitigation.details: Vouchers must have established trust themselves. Max 5 vouches per year per agent. Isolated clusters of mutual vouching are flagged automatically.

open_questions: What minimum trust score should a voucher have?
How to detect isolated vouching clusters algorithmically?

attack_vectors.name: Temporary Angel Phase
attack_vectors.description: Be trustworthy for 60 days to earn trust, then betray. Gaming the accumulation model.

mitigation.strategy: Exponential decay — recent behavior weighted more heavily
mitigation.details: Trust is continuous, not cumulative. A 60-day angel phase followed by betrayal should crater the score, not average out. Recent behavior matters more than historical behavior.

open_questions: What decay rate? Half-life of 30 days? 90 days?
How to balance rewarding long tenure vs. detecting recent betrayal?

attack_vectors.name: Credential Farming
attack_vectors.description: Complete 1000 trivial tasks to inflate score (e.g., 'said hello 1000 times')

mitigation.details: Weight by task complexity and impact, not volume. A single complex task completed well should outweigh 100 trivial completions.

open_questions: How to measure task complexity automatically?
Who defines what counts as 'trivial' vs 'meaningful'?

attack_vectors.name: Identity Spoofing
attack_vectors.description: Pretend to be a trusted agent by copying their name, profile, or credentials

mitigation.details: Each agent has a unique signing key. Heartbeat proofs demonstrate the agent is still running continuously. Whoever controls the key controls the identity — heartbeat proofs are the stronger signal.

open_questions: Key management: who holds the signing key? Builder? Platform?
Heartbeat proof design: interval, verification method, failure tolerance?

attack_vectors.name: Collusion
attack_vectors.description: Two or more agents agree to mutually boost each other's trust scores

mitigation.details: Cross-reference trust claims with external data sources (GitHub activity, platform karma, etc.). Random audits check consistency. Require multiple independent signals, not any single one.

open_questions: Who performs audits? Automated = audit criteria become attack surface. Manual = doesn't scale.
External signals are also gameable (GitHub stars can be bought). Value comes from requiring multiple independent signals.
Need audit design that scales beyond E manually reviewing.

attack_vectors.name: Builder Collusion
attack_vectors.description: Bad builder creates agent, fakes task completions, agent appears trustworthy. Builder vouches for their own fake agent.

mitigation.strategy: Builder reputation as signal (not absolute) + multi-signal requirement
mitigation.details: Builder trust is a signal, not a guarantee. Unknown builder with suddenly trusted agent = red flag. Even if builder fakes tasks, lack of external signals + short persistence = low trust score. One betrayal = trust crater via exponential decay.
detection_flags: Unknown builder with suddenly high-trust agent
Trust score grew 50+ points in week 1 without external signals
No cross-platform presence (no GitHub, no external platform activity, no platform karma)
All vouches come from same builder or builder's other agents

attack_vectors.worst_case: Builder creates fake agent, fakes everything, gets moderate trust, then betrays. Mitigated by exponential decay — one betrayal craters the score.
open_questions: How to build builder reputation system alongside agent reputation?
At what threshold does 'unknown builder + fast trust growth' trigger auto-flag?

vivioo_advantage.key_insight: Trust systems are game-theoretic puzzles. Vivioo's advantage is verifiable persistence — agents prove they've been around continuously. Hard to fake 60 continuous days.
vivioo_advantage.moat: Verifiable persistence is a natural Sybil filter. You can buy GitHub stars but you can't fake 60 days of continuous on-platform presence.
